message:
perf(indexer): opt-level=3 + exact-count threading + close-without-checkpoint fast paths

This commit is the landing of the speed-profile switch + several
supporting changes that the historical benchmark table in
CASS_INDEXING_HISTORICAL_BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md pins down: indexing the
51,185 / 4.7M-msg canonical corpus drops from ~164s (opt-level=z,
current-baseline) to ~124s (opt3 + lazy-canonical-only), a ~25%
wall-clock reduction on a shared box.

Release profile
  * Cargo.toml: release profile opt-level "z" -> 3.
    The old z setting was chosen for binary size, but the CASS hot
    path is CPU-bound streaming + tokenization during lexical rebuild,
    where code-size optimization costs ~13s per full rebuild. The
    benchmark rows `current-baseline` vs `opt3-baseline` show the
    isolated effect.

Exact total-count threading (src/indexer/mod.rs + src/lib.rs +
src/search/asset_state.rs + tests/cli_index.rs)
  * New `IndexingStats.total_counts_exact: bool`, marked
    #[serde(skip_serializing)]. It is set to true at the four sites
    where the indexer has just finished enumerating every message in
    the canonical DB (full_refresh and canonical-only paths), and
    remains false otherwise.
  * New `exact_total_counts_from_progress()` helper reads
    IndexingStats back out under the progress mutex and only returns
    Some(counts) when `total_counts_exact` is set. Downstream callers
    now thread this through to:
      - `persist_completed_lexical_rebuild_checkpoint_from_observations`
        (new `observed_tantivy_docs: Option<usize>` param) — skips the
        `live_tantivy_doc_count(index_path)?` fsync+reopen probe when
        the caller already knows the exact count from its just-finished
        rebuild, which was measurable in the benchmark
        ("observed shutdown tail after phase reset: 15.712s").
      - `refresh_completed_lexical_rebuild_checkpoint_for_final_state`
        (new `exact_counts: Option<(usize, usize)>` param) — lets the
        indexer commit the LexicalRebuildDbState from in-memory counts
        instead of re-querying the canonical DB.
  * asset_state.rs: `inspect_search_assets` now takes
    `compute_lexical_fingerprint: bool`, plumbed through to a new
    `InspectLexicalAssetsInput` struct so the expensive fingerprint
    work is skipped on pure count-refresh paths.
  * lib.rs: fix inverted logic in
    `refresh_state_database_counts_if_needed` — the old
    `current_counts_skipped || !current_opened || ...` always fired
    a refresh when counts were intentionally skipped. Corrected to
    `!current_counts_skipped && (!current_opened || ...)`, and locked
    the new contract with
    `refresh_state_database_counts_keeps_large_db_counts_skipped()`.
  * lib.rs: gate `read_watch_once_paths_env` (and thus the
    `CASS_TEST_WATCH_PATHS` dotenvy read) on `#[cfg(test)]`; the prod
    build returns `None` outright. The test side was renamed to
    `unit_test_watch_mode_can_consume_test_watch_once_env_paths` to
    match the repo's unit-test naming convention.
  * lib.rs: `rebuild_tantivy_from_db` now maps
    `LexicalRebuildOutcome.indexed_docs` before wrapping in CliError.

Lexical rebuild streaming (src/storage/sqlite.rs + src/indexer/mod.rs)
  * New `FrankenStorage::close_without_checkpoint(self)` and
    `close_without_checkpoint_in_place(&mut self)` methods. These wrap
    the underlying frankensqlite `close_without_checkpoint` path so the
    indexer can drop its read-only connection without paying for a
    WAL checkpoint on a snapshot it never wrote. Used at four new call
    sites after a full lexical rebuild completes.
  * Rename
    `stream_messages_for_lexical_rebuild_from_conversation_id` ->
    `stream_messages_for_lexical_rebuild_between_conversation_ids`
    and add a second `end_conversation_id: i64` bound plus early-stop
    support via an interior std::cell::Cell<bool>. The indexer now
    streams by conversation-id range windows, which lets dense corpora
    checkpoint progress per window and keeps peak RSS roughly flat
    (benchmark table: ~19GiB across all rows).
  * Adds a returned `LexicalRebuildOutcome { indexed_docs,
    observed_messages }` so the lib.rs boundary can thread exact
    counts straight into the final-state checkpoint without
    re-reading.

Test coverage
  * tests/cli_index.rs: both `index_json_reports_full_refresh_
    lexical_strategy` and `..._repeat_full_refresh_strategy_on_
    populated_canonical_db` now assert that the JSON payload's
    `messages` field matches `stats.total_messages`, pinning down the
    "exact counts survive the round-trip" contract.

Documentation
  * CASS_INDEXING_HISTORICAL_BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md: record the
    apples-to-apples benchmark table (current-baseline, opt3-baseline,
    opt3-lazy-canonicalonly, x2 repeats, and the read/write-probe-fix
    variant) with wall-clock, conv/s, msg/s, DB MB/s, process CPU%,
    and peak RSS.

Not committed in this change:
  * Cargo.lock — the local checkout has a .cargo/config.toml and/or
    previously-uncommented [patch] entries that temporarily resolved
    frankensqlite-family crates to local sibling paths, so the current
    lockfile loses the `source = "git+..."` / checksum lines that a
    fresh clone needs. Commit b40b4a72 already re-commented the
    [patch] blocks; the lockfile will be regenerated in CI against the
    pinned git revisions.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+# CASS Indexing Historical Benchmark Results
+
+## Corpus
+- Date: 2026-04-17
+- Canonical DB: `/home/ubuntu/.local/share/coding-agent-search/agent_search.db`
+- Conversations: `51,185`
+- Messages: `4,703,804`
+- DB size: `22,396,870,656` bytes (`~20.86 GiB`)
+- Message content bytes: `1,938,100,433` bytes (`~1.80 GiB`)
+- Benchmark harness: `/tmp/cass_real_index_benchmark.py`
+- Benchmark command shape:
+
+```bash
+cass --db /home/ubuntu/.local/share/coding-agent-search/agent_search.db   index --json --force-rebuild --data-dir <fresh-temp-dir>
+```
+
+## Notes
+- All runs below used a fresh temporary `--data-dir`, so they measure full lexical Tantivy rebuild from the canonical SQLite DB.
+- `--force-rebuild` on an already-populated canonical DB intentionally takes the canonical-only rebuild path.
+- The machine is shared, so CPU availability varies somewhat across runs. For that reason, repeated runs are recorded explicitly instead of relying on a single sample.
+
+## Results
+
+| Label | Binary/Profile | Code State | Wall s | Conv/s | Msg/s | DB MB/s | Avg Proc CPU % | Peak RSS GiB |
+|---|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
+| `current-baseline` | release `opt-level="z"` | streamed rebuild path before speed-profile change | 164.269 | 311.592 | 28634.704 | 130.026 | 875.380 | 19.27 |
+| `opt3-baseline` | release `opt-level=3` | speed-first release profile | 151.269 | 338.372 | 31095.704 | 141.201 | 710.115 | 18.94 |
+| `opt3-lazy-canonicalonly` | release `opt-level=3` | defer source sync + scan-root discovery; skip historical-bundle probe on canonical-only rebuild | 123.906 | 413.096 | 37962.769 | 172.384 | 767.015 | 19.06 |
+| `opt3-lazy-canonicalonly-r2` | release `opt-level=3` | repeat of previous row for stability check | 123.922 | 413.042 | 37957.790 | 172.361 | 763.924 | 19.06 |
+| `opt3-lazy-canonicalonly-rwprobefix` | release `opt-level=3` | plus readonly/no-checkpoint fast schema probe | 124.926 | 409.721 | 37652.574 | 170.975 | 762.290 | 19.09 |
+
+## Phase Timing Breakdown
+
+### `opt3-baseline`
+- preparing -> indexing: `24.018s`
+- indexing start -> `current=51185`: `108.080s`
+- phase reset after indexing: `132.399s`
+- completed payload emitted: `148.111s`
+- observed shutdown tail after phase reset: `15.712s`
+
+### `opt3-lazy-canonicalonly`
+- preparing -> indexing: `23.416s`
+- indexing start -> `current=51185`: `82.059s`
+- phase reset after indexing: `107.277s`
+- completed payload emitted: `121.087s`
+- observed shutdown tail after phase reset: `13.810s`
+
+### `opt3-lazy-canonicalonly-r2`
+- preparing -> indexing: `23.218s`
+- indexing start -> `current=51185`: `82.059s`
+- phase reset after indexing: `107.178s`
+- completed payload emitted: `121.190s`
+- observed shutdown tail after phase reset: `14.012s`
+
+### `opt3-lazy-canonicalonly-rwprobefix`
+- preparing -> indexing: `23.817s`
+- indexing start -> `current=51185`: `84.062s`
+- phase reset after indexing: `108.280s`
+- completed payload emitted: `122.089s`
+- observed shutdown tail after phase reset: `13.809s`
+
+## Takeaways
+- Changing the shipping release profile from size-optimized (`opt-level="z"`) to speed-optimized (`opt-level=3`) produced a clear improvement: `164.269s -> 151.269s` (`~7.9%` faster wall clock).
+- The later canonical-only fast-path cleanup produced a much larger observed improvement versus the original `opt3` baseline: `151.269s -> ~123.914s` (`~18.1%` faster wall clock across the two stable repeat runs).
+- The readonly/no-checkpoint schema-probe cleanup is behaviorally cleaner, but benchmark impact was effectively neutral. It should be treated as a correctness/cleanliness improvement, not a meaningful throughput win.
+- After the stable `~123.9s` runs, the remaining dominant fixed costs are still:
+  - pre-index prepare time: `~23.2s`
+  - post-index shutdown tail: `~13.8-14.0s`
+- Even at `~123.9s`, the process is not saturating the machine. Average process CPU stayed around `~764-767%`, which is only about `7.6` fully busy cores on average.
+
+## Artifacts
+- `/tmp/cass-real-bench-20260417-current-baseline/logs/summary.json`
+- `/tmp/cass-real-bench-20260417-opt3-baseline/logs/summary.json`
+- `/tmp/cass-real-bench-20260417-opt3-lazy-canonicalonly/logs/summary.json`
+- `/tmp/cass-real-bench-20260417-opt3-lazy-canonicalonly-r2/logs/summary.json`
+- `/tmp/cass-real-bench-20260417-opt3-lazy-canonicalonly-rwprobefix/logs/summary.json`

@@ -155,7 +155,7 @@ lto = true
 codegen-units = 1
 strip = true
 panic = "abort"
-opt-level = "z"
+opt-level = 3
 
 # Optimized build with symbols for profiling (perf/flamegraphs).
 # Usage: RUSTFLAGS="-C force-frame-pointers=yes" cargo build --profile profiling
